commands.review_summary:
The PR caps successful Discord `requestDiscord` response-body reads at 4 MiB and adds focused tests for avoiding `response.text()` and cancelling oversized streams.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +54. Total +57 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main calls unbounded `res.text()` on successful `requestDiscord` responses, and an oversized stream fixture can exercise that exact path. I did not run a live or loopback reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor changed-path proof and maintainer acceptance of the public response cap; this is not a safe autonomous repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: fix(discord): bound requestDiscord happy-path response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Discord channels under high message volume could cause unbounded memory growth: `requestDiscord` read the full success response via `res.text()` without any byte cap. Discord channel message lists, thread histories, and attachment manifests can reach significant sizes; without a cap the gateway process risks OOM.

## Why This Change Was Made

The error path in `requestDiscord` already used `readResponseTextLimited` (capped at `DISCORD_API_ERROR_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES` = 8 KiB) to prevent buffering oversized failure bodies. This PR applies the same helper to the success path using a separate `DISCORD_API_RESPONSE_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES` constant (4 MiB), which comfortably covers all valid Discord REST API payloads. Only `extensions/discord/src/api.ts` is touched; no shared infra files are modified.

## User Impact

Operators running busy Discord integrations no longer risk gateway OOM from unexpectedly large API responses. Normal Discord API payloads (well under 4 MiB) are unaffected.

## Evidence

Two new focused tests added to `extensions/discord/src/api.test.ts`:

**Mutation control** — verifies `readResponseTextLimited` is called instead of `res.text()`:
- Constructs a readable-stream response with valid JSON
- Mocks `res.text()` as a throwing spy
- Asserts `requestDiscord` returns the correct payload and the spy is never invoked

**Over-cap truncation** — verifies responses exceeding 4 MiB are bounded:
- Constructs a `ReadableStream` emitting > 4 MiB of data (prefix `{"items":[` + padding)
- Asserts `requestDiscord` throws `SyntaxError` (truncated JSON fails parse)
- Asserts stream `cancel()` was called, confirming reader was released early

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)   ← includes 14 pre-existing + 2 new bound tests
   Duration  127ms
```
